title: Code2Wav frame-dim SEQUENCE PARALLELISM for Qwen3-Omni (M*)

env_flags.MSTAR_CODE2WAV_SP_DEVICES: per-shard devices e.g. cuda:0,cuda:1 (absent=single-GPU shard mode)

design.where_shard: AFTER the cheap pre_transformer (sliding-window-72 attention runs on the FULL chunk so its context is exact), the EXPENSIVE upsample+decoder causal conv stack is sharded over contiguous frame ranges.
design.halo: Each non-first shard prepends `halo` real left-context frames; the conv stack is fully causal (left-pad-only convs, right-cropped transposed convs) so frames [a,b) depend only on input frames [a-R, b). Measured conv receptive field R ~= 4 frames; default halo=32 is 8x margin.
design.boundary_align: Decoder transposed convs lose a CONSTANT C=555 samples of causal warmup (output length = total_upsample*T - C, affine). Interior-shard trim = up*ctx - C (not up*ctx); shard 0 keeps everything. This makes the recombined waveform LENGTH and CONTENT exactly match the single-pass output (no off-by-C seam).
design.multi_device: Conv-stack weights are deep-copied onto each extra device; shard hidden slice -> .to(device) -> conv stack -> trim halo -> .to(base) -> concat. Talker+Code2Wav colocate on worker_0 (cuda:0); worker_0 can address cuda:1 (all GPUs visible, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES set process-wide pre-spawn), Thinker (worker_1, cuda:1) is idle during B=1 vocoding.
design.cuda_graph: SP path is cross-device+dynamic -> not graph-capturable; Code2WavSubmodule disables graph capture when SP on (runs eager).

parity_gate.method: Real Qwen3OmniMoeCode2Wav (random fp32 weights), single-pass vs sharded, identical weights/inputs. Full-waveform max-abs + cosine, plus +/-2400-sample boundary window.

exact_cases.exact_1gpu_2shard: len match exactly; max_abs ~1.1e-4 (fp32 conv-reassociation floor); cosine 0.99999997; seam_max_abs ~8e-5
exact_cases.exact_2gpu_2shard: len match exactly; max_abs ~1.0e-4; cosine 0.99999997; seam_max_abs ~8e-5
exact_cases.exact_2gpu_4shard: len match exactly; max_abs ~1.1e-4; cosine 0.99999997; 3 seams all ~8e-5

parity_gate.conclusion: Sharded waveform == single-device waveform to fp32 precision, including AT the shard boundary. Receptive field R~=4 frames; halo>=4 is exact. Default 32 is safe. NO seam/click.

microbench_verdict.method: Isolated vocoder forward timing (warmup 20, measure 100), T = input codec frames. T=50 is the production streaming chunk (codec_chunk 25 + left_context 25).
microbench_verdict.key_finding: The vocoder is LAUNCH-BOUND, not compute-bound: T=50->800 is 16x more frames but only 4.2x more time (single_compiled 11.29->47.14 ms). Splitting a launch-bound workload only MULTIPLIES kernel-launch overhead.
observations: SP NEVER beats the compiled baseline at any T (asymptotes to 0.95x at T=800, never reaches 1.0x).
2-GPU SP ~= 1-GPU SP at every T -> the second GPU adds nothing; the bottleneck is launch overhead/serialization, not GPU compute throughput.
torch.compile alone (already in the baseline) is the bigger lever: it fuses the launch-bound SnakeBeta+Conv+pad chains, exactly the cost SP cannot reduce.
Against the EAGER baseline, 2-GPU SP does help at large T (1.66x at T=800) -- but production uses the COMPILED path and streams tiny T=50 chunks.
microbench_verdict.verdict: Code2Wav frame-dim SP does NOT halve the vocoder cost. At the production T=50 streaming chunk it is 27-50% SLOWER than the compiled baseline. The premise (long audio => the vocoder dominates and is shardable) holds in aggregate, but each STREAMING chunk is only 50 frames -- below the regime where frame-splitting can amortize its doubled launch overhead, and the conv stack is launch-bound so the extra GPU is idle compute.

live_t2s_ab: see raw_live_t2s.json (assemble_ab.py) -- B=1 x50 OFF vs ON-2gpu E2E confirmation
